commit.message:
docs: update AGENTS.md dashboard navigation paths

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(dashboard): deduplicate @codemirror/state to fix metadata tab crash (#406)

Two versions of @codemirror/state (6.5.2 and 6.5.4) were installed
simultaneously, breaking instanceof checks and causing "Unrecognized
extension value" errors in the CodeMirror editor on the metadata tab.

Added a pnpm override to pin @codemirror/state to 6.5.4. Also added a
PR template rule to AGENTS.md.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(dashboard): deduplicate @codemirror/state to fix metadata tab crash (#406)

Two versions of @codemirror/state (6.5.2 and 6.5.4) were installed
simultaneously, breaking instanceof checks and causing "Unrecognized
extension value" errors in the CodeMirror editor on the metadata tab.

Added a pnpm override to pin @codemirror/state to 6.5.4. Also added a
PR template rule to AGENTS.md.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
chore: bump package-openclaw to 0.1.6 and core to 0.1.9 (#403)

* fix(openclaw): persist message dedup state to prevent duplicate uploads (#399)

capturedMessageCursor was a pure in-memory variable that reset to zero on
process restart or compaction, causing the entire transcript to be re-sent
to Acontext. This adds SHA-256-based dedup keyed by message index + content,
persisted per session to dataDir/.sent-messages.json.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(openclaw): address 9 code review issues + 5 follow-up fixes (#401)

* fix(openclaw): address 9 code review issues (3 critical, 6 important)

Fix race conditions, unbounded growth, path traversal, and other issues
identified during code review of the OpenClaw plugin.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(openclaw): fix init retry, load promise recovery, content encoding, and remove dead code

- Clear initPromise on _init() failure so bridge can retry instead of being permanently broken
- Clear sentMessagesLoadPromise/learnedSessionsLoadPromise on failure to allow retry
- Handle resp.content.type encoding (base64 vs text) in downloadSkillFiles
- Remove unused waitForProcessing method and messagesObservingStatus from interface
- Add 7 new tests covering all fixes (87 total pass)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(openclaw): atomic writes, download retry, URL binary fetch, summary error boundary, name collision detection

- downloadSkillFiles returns boolean success; failed skills preserve old updatedAt for retry
- URL-fetched files written as binary Buffer instead of UTF-8 text
- Each getSessionSummary call wrapped in try/catch so one failure doesn't abort the rest
- Extracted atomicWriteFile helper (write-then-rename) for persistSentMessages, persistLearnedSessions, writeManifest
- Sanitized name collisions detected and logged at sync time, second skill skipped
- Removed unused initPromise assignment in test helper
- Added 7 new tests covering all fixes (94 total)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(openclaw): fix concurrent retry, ENOENT handling, 3-way collision, and add 11 tests

- ensureSession/ensureLearningSpace: use .catch()/.then() pattern so
  concurrent callers can independently retry on failure
- loadSentMessages/loadLearnedSessions: distinguish ENOENT from corrupt
  JSON — warn on corruption instead of silently discarding dedup state
- _doSync: two-pass collision detection fixes 3-way collision bug and
  avoids downloading skills before detecting collisions
- _doSync: exclude colliding skill IDs from "deleted from remote" loop
  to prevent spurious warnings
- Null sentMessagesLoadPromise/learnedSessionsLoadPromise on success
  for symmetry with error branch

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(openclaw): address 9 code review issues + 5 follow-up fixes (#401)

* fix(openclaw): address 9 code review issues (3 critical, 6 important)

Fix race conditions, unbounded growth, path traversal, and other issues
identified during code review of the OpenClaw plugin.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(openclaw): fix init retry, load promise recovery, content encoding, and remove dead code

- Clear initPromise on _init() failure so bridge can retry instead of being permanently broken
- Clear sentMessagesLoadPromise/learnedSessionsLoadPromise on failure to allow retry
- Handle resp.content.type encoding (base64 vs text) in downloadSkillFiles
- Remove unused waitForProcessing method and messagesObservingStatus from interface
- Add 7 new tests covering all fixes (87 total pass)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(openclaw): atomic writes, download retry, URL binary fetch, summary error boundary, name collision detection

- downloadSkillFiles returns boolean success; failed skills preserve old updatedAt for retry
- URL-fetched files written as binary Buffer instead of UTF-8 text
- Each getSessionSummary call wrapped in try/catch so one failure doesn't abort the rest
- Extracted atomicWriteFile helper (write-then-rename) for persistSentMessages, persistLearnedSessions, writeManifest
- Sanitized name collisions detected and logged at sync time, second skill skipped
- Removed unused initPromise assignment in test helper
- Added 7 new tests covering all fixes (94 total)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(openclaw): fix concurrent retry, ENOENT handling, 3-way collision, and add 11 tests

- ensureSession/ensureLearningSpace: use .catch()/.then() pattern so
  concurrent callers can independently retry on failure
- loadSentMessages/loadLearnedSessions: distinguish ENOENT from corrupt
  JSON — warn on corruption instead of silently discarding dedup state
- _doSync: two-pass collision detection fixes 3-way collision bug and
  avoids downloading skills before detecting collisions
- _doSync: exclude colliding skill IDs from "deleted from remote" loop
  to prevent spurious warnings
- Null sentMessagesLoadPromise/learnedSessionsLoadPromise on success
  for symmetry with error branch

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix(openclaw): persist message dedup state to prevent duplicate uploads (#399)

capturedMessageCursor was a pure in-memory variable that reset to zero on
process restart or compaction, causing the entire transcript to be re-sent
to Acontext. This adds SHA-256-based dedup keyed by message index + content,
persisted per session to dataDir/.sent-messages.json.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(cli): require explicit flags for API key operations in non-TTY mode (#389)

Previously, `projects select --project <id>` would silently rotate the
API key when no local key existed in non-TTY mode, which could
invalidate keys used by other clients. Now non-TTY mode requires
explicit `--api-key` or `--rotate` flags and prints guidance when
neither is provided.

commit.message:
fix(api,core): bump skill updated_at on file mutations for incremental sync (#386)

* fix(api,core): bump skill updated_at on file mutations for incremental sync

When skill files are added, edited, deleted, or moved, the parent
AgentSkill.updated_at was not being updated. This caused incremental-sync
clients (e.g. OpenClaw plugin) to miss file changes entirely since they
compare remote vs local timestamps to decide whether to re-download.

CORE: add touch_skill_updated_at() helper and call it from
create_skill_file, delete_skill_file, mv_skill_file, and
str_replace_skill_file handlers after successful artifact operations.

API: add TouchUpdatedAtByDiskID to AgentSkillsRepo, inject into
ArtifactService, and call (best-effort) after Create, CreateFromBytes,
DeleteByPath, and UpdateArtifactMetaByPath.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(dashboard): coerce chart tooltip dataKey to string for React key

Same fix as dashboard/components/ui/chart.tsx — recharts types allow
dataKey to be a function, which is not a valid React key.

fix(dashboard): cast tooltip props to fix formatter type mismatch

Recharts Tooltip content callback provides props with a generic
Formatter<ValueType, NameType> type that is incompatible with
ChartTooltipContent's narrower formatter signature. Cast spread props
via React.ComponentProps to resolve the type error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(dashboard): fix eslint error and TypeScript build error

- code-editor.tsx: wrap synchronous setState in queueMicrotask to satisfy
  react-hooks/set-state-in-effect rule
- chart.tsx: coerce item.dataKey to string for React key prop since recharts
  types allow it to be a function

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(dashboard): remove explicit CustomTooltipProps type annotation to fix build

The recharts Tooltip `content` prop expects `ContentType<ValueType, NameType>`,
which is incompatible with the explicit `CustomTooltipProps` annotation due to
a `formatter` property mismatch. Removing the annotation lets TypeScript infer
the correct type while still passing props to ChartTooltipContent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(cli): add Dashboard bridge — login, dash commands, and project management (#367)

* feat(cli): add Dashboard bridge — login, dash commands, and project management

Add browser OAuth login, Dashboard API client, and full CRUD commands
for sessions, disks, skills, spaces, messages, artifacts, users, and
projects. Enables AI agents to authenticate and operate Dashboard
resources via CLI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(cli): streamline login with project selection and agent guidance

- Login TTY mode: OAuth → org/project selection → API key save in one step
- Login non-TTY mode: print step-by-step guidance for agents
- Add `dash projects select` command (--project flag for non-interactive)
- Add `dash projects create --org` flag with Supabase RPC integration
- Fix admin API base URL (admin.acontext.app vs api.acontext.app)
- Add Supabase RPC helpers: CreateOrganization, LinkProjectToOrg
- Remove obsolete commands: set-key, rotate-key, use
- Update requireClient() error messages

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cli): resolve golangci-lint errcheck and staticcheck issues

- Add `_ =` for intentionally ignored error returns (Close, Flush, MarkFlagRequired, etc.)
- Lowercase error strings per staticcheck ST1005
- Fix all 46 errcheck/staticcheck lint errors across cli codebase

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(cli): replace localhost callback with polling-based login

Switch from localhost HTTP callback to Supabase Edge Function polling
for OAuth token retrieval. This enables CLI login from remote
environments (SSH, containers) where localhost is unreachable.

- Remove localhost listener and background process fork
- Add LoginInteractive (TTY: poll with spinner) and LoginNonInteractive
  (non-TTY: print URL, save state, exit)
- Add `login --poll` for agents to retrieve tokens after user completes
  browser login
- Add ClaimCLISession to poll claim-cli-session Edge Function

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(cli): add missing dash subcommands (get, upload)

- sessions get: view single session details
- disks get: view single disk details
- spaces get: view single learning space details
- artifacts upload: upload a file to a disk with optional --path flag
- Add corresponding API methods: GetSession, GetDisk, UploadArtifact

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cli): harden dashboard bridge — config validation, poll resilience, URL escaping

- Validate SupabaseURL/SupabaseAnonKey before login to fail early on misconfigured builds
- Retry transient network errors in pollForSession instead of aborting immediately
- Use shorter 15s timeout for LoginPoll (agent --poll mode) instead of 120s
- Escape query params in GetSession/GetDisk via url.Values to prevent injection
- Fix PersistentPreRunE to chain parent's error-returning hook variant
- Clarify in AGENTS.md that plans/ is gitignored and local-only

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: preserve image_url parts in OpenAI converter on message retrieval (#357)

The OpenAI converter silently dropped image parts when retrieving
multimodal messages. URL-based images (stored in Part.Meta["url"])
were lost because the converter only checked GetAssetURL() which
returns empty for non-uploaded assets. Added Meta URL fallback
matching the Anthropic and Gemini converters.

Also fixed GetAssetURL key mismatch: lookup used asset.S3Key but
the publicURLs map is keyed by asset.SHA256.

Made-with: Cursor

multimodal messages. URL-based images (stored in Part.Meta["url"])

returns empty for non-uploaded assets. Added Meta URL fallback

commit.message:
feat: add @acontext/openclaw plugin for OpenClaw agents (#348)

* feat: add @acontext/openclaw plugin for OpenClaw agents

Implements the Acontext skill memory plugin for OpenClaw with:

- Auto-Capture: stores agent turns to Acontext sessions, triggers
  CORE task extraction via flush
- Auto-Recall: injects learned skill file contents + recent task
  summaries into prependContext before each agent turn
- Auto-Learn: triggers Learning Space skill distillation when
  enough conversation accumulates (non-blocking background)
- Local Skill Cache: downloads skill .md files to local disk via
  api.resolvePath(), reads from filesystem on subsequent recalls
  with manifest-based staleness (30 min); zero API calls after sync
- 4 Agent Tools: acontext_search_skills, acontext_read_skill,
  acontext_session_history, acontext_learn_now
- CLI: openclaw acontext skills, openclaw acontext stats
- AcontextBridge with injected logger for all error paths

Package: src/packages/openclaw/ (npm: @acontext/openclaw)
Made-with: Cursor

* test: add 39 unit tests for @acontext/openclaw plugin

- Config parsing: env var interpolation, validation, defaults, error cases
- Helper functions: truncateByTokenEstimate, stripInjectedContext
- Plugin registration: tools, hooks, CLI, service with feature toggles
- Auto-recall/capture hooks: skip conditions, error resilience
- Mock infrastructure: openclaw/plugin-sdk and @acontext/acontext stubs

Made-with: Cursor

* ci: add CI workflows for @acontext/openclaw

- package-test-openclaw.yaml: validates package.json, extension
  entry points, TypeScript type check (tsc --noEmit), unit tests,
  plugin structure verification, and dry-run pack on PRs to dev
- package-release-openclaw.yaml: tag-triggered npm publish with
  version verification and GitHub Release creation

Made-with: Cursor

* docs: add OpenClaw integration page to documentation

Add integrations/openclaw.mdx with quick start, configuration,
agent tools, CLI commands, and recall explanation. Register in
docs.json navigation and integrations intro card grid.

Made-with: Cursor

* chore: add dependabot config for @acontext/openclaw

Add npm ecosystem entry for src/packages/openclaw/ to keep
dependencies up to date, matching the existing sandbox-cloudflare
pattern.

Made-with: Cursor

* chore(openclaw): add files field to exclude tests from npm package

commit.message:
rebrand: rewrite landing page and README for skill memory narrative (#341)

* rebrand(landingpage): update content narrative from memory stack to skill memory

Rewrite landing page content to shift positioning from "Agent Memory Stack"
(three-layer architecture) to "Skill Memory" (Learn, Write, Reuse):

- Update metadata: title, description, keywords, OpenGraph, Twitter cards
- Hero: swap tagline/H1, add badge style, replace CTAs with feature tags
- Overview: rename to "How Skill Memory Works", update tab content and icons
- Quickstart: consolidate to single "Skill Memory" tab with updated SDK examples
- Features: reduce to two cards ("Skill Memory" + "SDKs & Integrations")

Made-with: Cursor

* rebrand(landingpage): rework demo animations for Learn, Write, Reuse narrative

Rewrite three demo animations to align with the new skill memory positioning:

- StoreDemo → LearnDemo: agent conversation, task extraction, learning queue
- ObserveDemo → WriteDemo: skill distillation, Markdown preview with typing
- SkillsDemo → ReuseDemo: agent calls get_skill/get_skill_file, applies SOPs
- DashboardDemo: update insight notification to skill reuse metrics

Made-with: Cursor

* rebrand(readme): rewrite README to focus on skill memory positioning

Replace top sections of README with new skill memory narrative:

- What Is Acontext: open-source agent skills as a memory layer
- Who Is It For: developers with agents that repeat mistakes
- Why Not Other Memory: comparison table vs Mem0/Zep/RAG
- What Acontext Does: Markdown-native, schema design, tool-based recall, ZIP export
- How It Works: Store and Recall mermaid flow diagrams
- Quickstart: rename to "Skill Memory in Action" with learn→reuse example
- Document section: update copy to reference skill memory

Made-with: Cursor

* rebrand(docs): rename three-layer terminology to skill memory narrative

Update all 21 docs files to replace old naming convention:
- "Short-term Memory" → "Session Storage"
- "Mid-term State" → "Task Tracking"
- "Long-term Skill" → "Skill Memory"
- "agent memory stack" → "skill memory for AI agents"

Changes include:
- docs.json: rename 3 navigation groups
- index.mdx: rewrite intro and card titles
- api-reference/introduction.mdx: update description
- 6 page titles/body: store/overview, observe/whatis, learn/quick,
  learn/custom-memory, learn/learning-spaces, store/skill
- 5 integration files: rename "Mid-term State" cards to "Task Tracking"
- 7 files: rename "Long-term Skill" cards to "Skill Memory"
- claude-agent.mdx: update body text reference
- memory-stack.svg: update card labels

Made-with: Cursor

fix(ts-sdk): convert timeout from seconds to milliseconds in agent tools (#320)

* fix(ts-sdk): convert timeout from seconds to milliseconds in agent tools

BashTool and TextEditorTool accept timeout in seconds (matching the LLM-facing
API contract and the Python SDK convention), but pass the value directly to
SandboxesAPI.execCommand which feeds it into setTimeout (milliseconds).
This causes requests to abort ~1000x earlier than intended.

commit.message:
feat(session): add copy session functionality with SDK support (#280)

* feat(session): add fork session functionality with SDK support

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* fix(py): Remove redundant f-string prefixes in fork_session_usage

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* fix(api): Add ForkSession to MockSessionRepo for service tests

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* Add test cases covering transaction rollback, concurrent access, size limits, and data integrity for fork session functionality, update openapi doc

Co-Authored-By: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* fix(review): address PR #280 code review feedback

Critical fixes:
- Remove unused Redis dependency from RouterDeps and main.go
- Replace strings.Contains error matching with typed errors.Is(err, repo.ErrSessionTooLarge)
- Fix BatchIncrementAssetRefs to use tx (atomic with session/message creation)
- Move S3 downloads outside transaction to avoid holding lock during network I/O

Performance:
- Batch message and task INSERTs with CreateInBatches instead of per-row INSERTs

Nits:
- Revert unrelated whitespace alignment change in service/session.go PartIn struct
- Fix MaxForkableMessages handler comment to proper alias declaration
- Fix TypeScript JSDoc: "UUID v4" → "UUID (v1–v5)" to match regex
- Fix Python docstring: ValueError/RuntimeError → APIError

Tests:
- Replace placeholder "transaction rollback on message creation failure" test
  with meaningful "fork with single message succeeds" test
- Use CreateInBatches for size limit test setup (avoid 5001 sequential INSERTs)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* rename(session): rename fork to copy across all modules

Rename all "fork session" terminology to "copy session" for clarity:
- Rename API route /fork -> /copy, handler/service/repo methods
- Rename SDK methods .fork() -> .copy() in Python and TypeScript SDKs
- Rename docs page fork_session.mdx -> copy_session.mdx
- Rename example file fork_session_usage.py -> copy_session_usage.py
- Update docs navigation to reference copy_session

commit.message:
fix(ci): harden and optimize GitHub Actions workflows (#296)

* chore(ci): rename publish-chart.yml to .yaml for consistency

All other workflow files use the .yaml extension. Standardize
this one to match the project convention.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): optimize test and scan workflows

Unify Python version to 3.13, add permissions to security-reusable,
optimize concurrency and path filters for test workflows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): add CLI gomod ecosystem to dependabot config

* chore(ci): harden workflows with permissions, SHA pins, and timeouts

Add least-privilege permissions and CODEOWNERS, pin all GitHub Actions
to commit SHAs for supply chain security, and add timeout-minutes to
all jobs to prevent runaway costs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(ci): optimize release workflows with reusable Docker workflow

Optimize release workflows and extract shared Docker build-push logic
into _reusable-docker-release.yaml, refactoring core, api, and ui
release workflows to call it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): fix cache-dependency-path and artifact upload guards

Add cache-dependency-path to all setup-go, setup-python, and setup-node
steps to prevent cross-module cache invalidation. Add if: !cancelled()
to artifact upload steps. Add scope to Docker GHA cache to prevent
cross-image eviction.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

releases.name: sdk-ts/v0.1.16
releases.body:
# TypeScript SDK v0.1.16

Published to npm as @acontext/acontext@0.1.16

readme_html:
What is Acontext?

Acontext is an open-source skill memory layer for AI agents. It automatically captures learnings from agent runs and stores them as Markdown files — files you can read, edit, and share across agents, LLMs, and frameworks.

If you want the agent you build to learn from its mistakes and reuse what worked — without opaque memory polluting your context — give Acontext a try.

Skill is All You Need

Agent memory is getting increasingly complicated🤢 — hard to understand, hard to debug, and hard for users to inspect or correct. Acontext takes a different approach: if agent skills can represent every piece of knowledge an agent needs as simple files, so can the memory.

  • Acontext builds memory in the agent skills format, so everyone can see and understand what the memory actually contains.
  • Skill is Memory, Memory is Skill. Whether a skill comes from one you downloaded from Clawhub or one you created yourself, Acontext can follow it and evolve it over time.

The Philosophy of Acontext

  • Plain file, any framework — Skill memories are Markdown files. Use them with LangGraph, Claude, AI SDK, or anything that reads files. No embeddings, no API lock-in. Git, grep, and mount to the sandbox.
  • You design the structure — Attach more skills to define the schema, naming, and file layout of the memory. For example: one file per contact, one per project by uploading a working context skill.
  • Progressive disclosure, not search — The agent can use get_skill and get_skill_file to fetch what it needs. Retrieval is by tool use and reasoning, not semantic top-k.
  • Download as ZIP, reuse anywhere — Export skill files as ZIP. Run locally, in another agent, or with another LLM. No vendor lock-in; no re-embedding or migration step.

How It Works

Store — How skills get memorized?

flowchart LR
  A[Session messages] --> C[Task complete/failed]
  C --> D[Distillation]
  D --> E[Skill Agent]
  E --> F[Update Skills]
  • Session messages — Conversation (and optionally tool calls, artifacts) is the raw input. Tasks are extracted from the message stream automatically (or inferred from explicit outcome reporting).
  • Task complete or failed — When a task is marked done or failed (e.g. by agent report or automatic detection), that outcome is the trigger for learning.
  • Distillation — An LLM pass infers from the conversation and execution trace what worked, what failed, and user preferences.
  • Skill Agent — Decides where to store (existing skill or new) and writes according to your SKILL.md schema.
  • Update Skills — Skills are updated. You define the structure in SKILL.md; the system does extraction, routing, and writing.

Recall — How the agent uses skills on the next run

flowchart LR
  E[Any Agent] --> F[list_skills/get_skill]
  F --> G[Appear in context]

Give your agent Skill Content Tools (get_skill, get_skill_file). The agent decides what it needs, calls the tools, and gets the skill content. No embedding search — progressive disclosure, agent in the loop.

🚀 Step-by-step Quickstart

Connect to Acontext

  1. Go to Acontext.io, claim your free credits.
  2. Go through a one-click onboarding to get your API Key (starts with sk-ac)

We have an acontext-cli to help you do a quick proof-of-concept. Download it first in your terminal:

curl -fsSL https://install.acontext.io | sh

You should have docker installed and an OpenAI API Key to start an Acontext backend on your computer:

mkdir acontext_server && cd acontext_server
acontext server up

Make sure your LLM has the ability to call tools. By default, Acontext will use gpt-4.1.

acontext server up will create/use .env and config.yaml for Acontext, and create a db folder to persist data.

Once it's done, you can access the following endpoints:

  • Acontext API Base URL: http://localhost:8029/api/v1
  • Acontext Dashboard: http://localhost:3000/

Install SDKs

We're maintaining Python and Typescript SDKs. The snippets below are using Python.

Click the doc link to see TS SDK Quickstart.

pip install acontext

Initialize Client

import os
from acontext import AcontextClient

# For cloud:
client = AcontextClient(
    api_key=os.getenv("ACONTEXT_API_KEY"),
)

# For self-hosted:
client = AcontextClient(
    base_url="http://localhost:8029/api/v1",
    api_key="sk-ac-your-root-api-bearer-token",
)

Skill Memory in Action

Create a learning space, attach a session, and let the agent learn — skills are written as Markdown files automatically.

from acontext import AcontextClient

client = AcontextClient(api_key="sk-ac-...")

# Create a learning space and attach a session
space = client.learning_spaces.create()
session = client.sessions.create()
client.learning_spaces.learn(space.id, session_id=session.id)

# Run your agent, store messages — when tasks complete, learning runs automatically
client.sessions.store_message(session.id, blob={"role": "user", "content": "My name is Gus"})
client.sessions.store_message(session.id, blob={"role": "assistant", "content": "Hi Gus! How can I help you today?"})
# ... agent runs ...

# List learned skills (Markdown files)
client.learning_spaces.wait_for_learning(space.id, session_id=session.id)
skills = client.learning_spaces.list_skills(space.id)

# Download all skill files to a local directory
for skill in skills:
    client.skills.download(skill_id=skill.id, path=f"./skills/{skill.name}")

wait_for_learning is a blocking helper for demo purposes. In production, task extraction and learning run in the background automatically — your agent never waits.

More Features

  • Context Engineering — Compress context with summaries and edit strategies
  • Disk — Virtual, persistent filesystem for agents
  • Sandbox — Isolated code execution with bash, Python, and mountable skills
  • Agent Tools — Disk tools, sandbox tools, and skill tools for LLM function calling

🧐 Use Acontext to Build Agents

Download end-to-end scripts with acontext:

Python

acontext create my-proj --template-path "python/openai-basic"

More examples on Python:

  • python/openai-agent-basic: openai agent sdk template
  • python/openai-agent-artifacts: agent can edit and download artifacts
  • python/claude-agent-sdk: claude agent sdk with ClaudeAgentStorage
  • python/agno-basic: agno framework template
  • python/smolagents-basic: smolagents (huggingface) template
  • python/interactive-agent-skill: interactive sandbox with mountable agent skills

Typescript

acontext create my-proj --template-path "typescript/openai-basic"

More examples on Typescript:

  • typescript/vercel-ai-basic: agent in @vercel/ai-sdk
  • typescript/claude-agent-sdk: claude agent sdk with ClaudeAgentStorage
  • typescript/interactive-agent-skill: interactive sandbox with mountable agent skills

[!NOTE]

Check our example repo for more templates: Acontext-Examples.

We're cooking more full-stack Agent Applications! Tell us what you want!

🔍 Documentation

To learn more about skill memory and what Acontext can do, visit our docs or start with What is Skill Memory?

❤️ Stay Updated

Star Acontext on GitHub to support us and receive instant notifications.

🏗️ Architecture

graph TB
    subgraph "Client Layer"
        PY["pip install acontext"]
        TS["npm i @acontext/acontext"]
    end
    
    subgraph "Acontext Backend"
